examples: In 2008, London created its Low Emission Zone (LEZ) to encourage the most polluting heavy diesel vehicles driving in the city to become cleaner.
In January, 2012 the LEZ emissions standards became more stringent, and tens of thousands of trucks and buses were retrofitted with filters and other systems to enable them to comply.
He said the introduction of the Congestion Charge in 2003 and the Low Emission Zone (LEZ) in 2008 had only made a "tiny difference" to air quality in the capital.
